zFfontTools.misc.fixedTools.py -- tools for working with fixed numbers.
print_function
division
absolute_import)
otRound
fixedToFloat
floatToFixed
floatToFixedToFloat
ensureVersionIsLong
versionToFixedc
Round float value to nearest integer towards +Infinity.
	For fractional values of 0.5 and higher, take the next higher integer;
	for other fractional values, truncate.
	https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/otvaroverview
	https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/1248#issuecomment-383198166
mathZ
floor
value
=/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/fontTools/misc/fixedTools.pyr
Converts a fixed-point number to a float, choosing the float
	that has the shortest decimal reprentation.  Eg. to convert a
	fixed number in a 2.14 format, use precisionBits=14.  This is
	pretty slow compared to a simple division.  Use sporadically.
	precisionBits is only supported up to 16.
%.8f
%%.%df)
float
round
range
find)	r
precisionBits
scaleZ
periodr
zvConverts a float to a fixed-point number given the number of
	precisionBits.  Ie. round(value * (1<<precisionBits)).
Converts a float to a fixed-point number given the number of
	precisionBits, round it, then convert it back to float again.
	Ie. round(value * (1<<precisionBits)) / (1<<precisionBits)
	Note: this is *not* equivalent to fixedToFloat(floatToFixed(value)),
	which would return the shortest representation of the rounded value.
zlEnsure a table version is an unsigned long (unsigned short major,
	unsigned short minor) instead of a float.i
zDTable version value is a float: %.4f; fix to use hex instead: 0x%08x)
logZ
warning)
newValuer
z#Converts a table version to a fixed
